post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 193: Worm Mafia
Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not refer to private conversations with the moderator in any way unless given permission.
    • 1.5: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.6: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.7: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.8: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.9: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.10: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 336 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 120 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 193 and mymathkid is the comod
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Sample VT PM
Quote:
You are a Citizen of Brockton Bay. You have no power other than your vote.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.

This mafia game is based off of the web novel Worm by Wildbow
https://parahumans.wordpress.com/

Story
The city of Brockton Bay, located on the east coast of the United States, was once a prosperous port city. But economic decline brought hard times, and when riots crippled the shipping industry, the city sunk into its darkest times. The increasing numbers of parahumans, colloquially known as capes, people with special powers, only contributed to the destabilization of the world. With poverty rampant, gangs proliferated and large portions of the city were de facto under the control of those gangs. There is the Empire 88, a white supremacist gang that seeks to rid the city of those it finds impure. There is the Azn Bad Boys, an Asian gang that engages in human trafficking. Despite this, little has been done to stop them as their feared parahuman leader known as Lung deters attackers. Then there are the Merchants, a gang filled with the scum of the earth who peddle drugs. Yet, their reach is considerable, despite the incompetence of their ordinary members.

The primary force defending the law is the Parahuman Response Team, along with the parahuman heroes in the Protectorate East North East. But their resources are few and with every day the city slips further. With the national organization refusing to increase funding and declining to send aid, the PRT and Protectorate ENE are on their own...

***

A mysterious figure wearing a fedora stood atop the roof of a building in Brockton Bay, looking out over the city. For a long time, there were minimal actions taken to interfere in the city so that the natural effects of neglect could be observed. Yet, it is through conflict that the true character of men is revealed, and Brockton Bay had simmered in stagnation for too long. It was time to take the experiment to the next level. Clearly, waiting would only delay the results. The Terminus Project would see its conclusion soon.

All conditions have been met and the necessary events have been set in motion. "Door", the mysterious individual muttered. Moments later, the roof was empty.

***

After Lung had been captured, the PRT expected that there would be an attempt by the ABB to break him out. They did not expect this. The Rig had been blown up and several of the Protectorate heroes had died along with countless PRT personnel and Lung was broken out. The creations of the ABB's new bomb tinker had proven to be far more potent than expected, and due to an unfortunate series of circumstances, people being in the wrong place at the wrong time, casualties had been devastating.

While the breakout of prisoners were common in Brockton Bay, with the phrase "revolving door prison" being used, this recent event represented an unprecedented escalation. With this, the heroes were forced to do what they had always been seeking to avoid, confronting the gangs directly to root them out using lethal force.

The ABB's bomb tinker was successfully eliminated, though not without cost. However, this caused even greater problems. With the increased use of force by the PRT and Protectorate, the other gangs became frightened, especially when one of the E88's capes died mysteriously. The gangs went on the offensive and now the city was embroiled in war.

The fate of the city will be decided by the victor of this war.


There will be a 2 day consultation period before the game begins, where players can ask clarification questions about their roles. Players with nightchat may talk to their partners during this time. Confirm you are a participant in this game by posting in your night chat, and if you are a role without one, simply send a message to the moderator to confirm.
Do not post until day begins.

Day 1 begins on 12:00:00 EST 28 December 2019.

post_rendered:
Alright short guide for cj(and other first/second timers). Why am I giving this I'm not even qualified.

1. Read the game. This is very important. Read every word and don't skim
2. Sound sure of yourself when you post.
3. Don't just blindly agree with what an experienced player says. Think it through.
4. If you want to post, post something new or a new insight on an old topic and don't just say "I agree" to everything you see
5. Make sure everything you say has some good reason behind it. Pretend you are someone else reading your post and see if you can catch any holes in your reasoning.
6. TRY. Put effort into the game.

This is way easier said than done. I can't even do all of this. But at least try.
post_canonical:

Come on I’m saying generally not all the freaking time. You guys are hyperbolizing everything I say. There are freaks of nature out there like Jeff who can do all these things, but for the vast majority of people, they don’t. Like just take a look at 191, 188, 186 (games I paid attention to so that’s why I’m referencing them), scum just sat in the background for the most part, and me and Nd were able to call that out in 191, but 188 and 186 town was just focusing on the person who drew the most attention. Like seriously stop focusing on the exception rather than reality. Sitting in the background especially in a game like this is definitely optimal for scum, I have no idea mathjay why you thing that strat is suboptimal. Not only do scum have to avoid a hang they have to avoid a NK from the other factions so staying invisible is how to stay alive. That’s why I find the likes of mathjay, tikki, and kbwuf more townie atm early on even though I thoroughly disagree with basically everything they’re all saying. Also, kbwufs play seems pretty similar to 186 where he was town which gives me a bigger town vibe for him. Now don’t go say “Oh, RTG and kbwuf are probably w/w because RTG is trying to clear kbwuf” like you have with both my other strong townreads. I could totally be wrong on 1 of these 3 but with my track record I feel confident in myself that I am not.

@ND I’m just examining what a player with Smart’s experience would think and how would he act. Now this play is not optimal as town or scum, but it makes much more sense as town. As town it could have just slipped out without thinking feeling the pressure of being an important town PR. This happens to a lot of newbies and Smart has never been town let alone a town PR before. However as mafia, Smart would have had to think about this play. As mafia, claiming PR usually only happens if a hang seems imminent which it wasn’t. Also, while Smart is thinking about claiming PR I’d think it would dawn on him if he claimed that he would draw attention to himself from other scum teams which in the two days of discussion in QTs should have been a talking point between members not to attract attention to oneself. So there’s my full reasoning why I believe Smart and is good enough to give him a very strong townread for me.

amalya wrote:
Also this is only my second game of Mafia on AoPS, so I am not very familiar with all the terms you use... what does scummy mean, and scumlean? And MY? And all the others?????????
A. Scummy means mafia-like behavior.
B. Scumlean means you are leaning towards the idea that someone is mafia/scum.
C. MY is an abbreviation for MY-2 (a player in this game).
D. The others [the underlined terms] are all either abbreviations or names of players in the game.
E. Null means you think the person could either be mafia or town.
F. Friendly reminder not to edit your posts.

There are two types of players: players who are fine with PLs and players who are not.

Posted On Apr 20th 2011:
Skidmark has just declared victory over his enemies in Brockton Bay. The Merchants are openly walking through the streets and no one is doing anything to stop them except some lone shooters that get taken down quickly. Perhaps with the PRT in chaos, the Merchants feel confident enough to do what they want. It is unclear what will happen after this.

The US government has put out a statement that they would give their full support to the city government of Brockton Bay. What this support would entail has been left unclear, nor has any immediate action been taken.

Role PMs
You are Emily Piggot, the director of the PRT ENE. You are the leader of the parahuman law enforcement in Brockton Bay. You have 5 PRT Troopers under your command, and each night you may give an order for one of them to kill someone. Unfortunately, there are likely moles among them, and some might fail to carry out their orders. The PRT troopers are ().

You can also give orders to the parahumans under your command, Armsmaster (), Battery (), and Clockblocker(). Their abilities are as follows.

<insert>

Note that each of them can willingly disobey your order and perform their own action at most once in the game. You will be informed when this occurs.

You must submit all orders in the 48 hours after the start of night.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Colin Wallis, also known as Armsmaster, a parahuman in the Protectorate ENE. You are an expert Tinker with a specialization in miniaturization who has made a large variety of powerful gear for yourself. Your power armor can allow you to survive one attack when it is deployed. Also, you have a wide variety of tools embedded within your halberd. You have an investigation tool (Cop), electronic tracker (Tracker), tranquilizer (Roleblocker), and monomolecular blade (Vigilante). One device can be used each night. Each device may only be used once after each maintenance. Once you perform actions on two nights you must perform maintenance on your equipment, which will fix it (restore 1-shot bulletproof). During the night you perform maintenance your equipment is not operational.

You take orders from the director of the PRT. If orders are sent do you, you will receive them before the 72nd hour after night begins. Once during the game you may willingly disobey your order and perform a different action.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.




You are Battery, a parahuman in the Protectorate ENE. Your power allows you to charge your ability and then use it. Each night you may either charge your ability, use your ability, or do nothing. Your ability must be charged before use. Using your ability will cause you to become immune to night actions during that night and you are able to kill someone.

You take orders from the director of the PRT. If orders are sent do you, you will receive them before the 72nd hour after night begins. Once during the game you may willingly disobey your order and perform a different action.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Clockblocker, a parahuman in the Wards ENE. Each night you may target someone with your ability and freeze them. They will be unable to take actions that night and they cannot be killed.

You take orders from the director of the PRT. If orders are sent do you, you will receive them before the 72nd hour after night begins. Once during the game you may willingly disobey your order and perform a different action.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are a PRT trooper, serving the director of the PRT ENE. You can kill someone during the night, but only if the director orders you to.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Victoria Dallon, also known as Glory Girl, a member of the independent hero team New Wave. Each night you can choose someone to kill. Also, your forcefield will protect you from being attacked, but not if you are attacked more than once on a single night or if you are attacked the night after a night where your forcefield was used.

During the night you can talk with your sister Amy () here.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Amy Dallon, also known as Panacea, a member of the independent hero team New Wave. Each night you can choose someone other than yourself to heal.

During the night you can talk with your sister Victoria () here.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.


You are a Citizen of Brockton Bay. You have no power other than your vote.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Kaiser, the leader of the Empire 88, the greatest gang in Brockton Bay. Each night you may kill someone. This kill is independent from the factional kill for the E88, but you yourself cannot perform both in a single night. During the night you may speak with Othala () and PRT Trooper – E88 mole () here.

You win when all threats to the E88’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are Othala, a parahuman member of the Empire 88. Each night you may target someone other than yourself and protect them from being killed. During the night you may speak with Kaiser () and PRT Trooper – E88 mole () here.

You win when all threats to the E88’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are a PRT Trooper, and also a mole for the Empire 88. Having risen from being a common grunt, you are now a valued spy. However, times may call for you to be used for common grunt work like killing. The PRT director can order you to kill someone during the night. You have the ability to not carry out the order, and the director will not be notified of this. The order will be received before 72 hours after night begins. If you choose to perform the ordered kill then you cannot also perform the E88 factional kill.

During the night you may speak with Kaiser () and Othala () here.

You win when all threats to the E88’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are Lung, the leader of the Azn Bad Boys, the greatest gang in Brockton Bay. Each night you may kill someone. This kill is independent from the factional kill for the ABB, but you yourself cannot perform both in a single night. In addition, if you are targeted by at least two kills during a single night, you will survive both and instead kill everyone who attacked you. During the night you may speak with ABB grunt () and PRT Trooper – ABB mole () here.

You win when all threats to the ABB’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are an ABB grunt, a common goon working for the Azn Bad Boys. Each night you may perform the ABB factional kill, if it is assigned to you. During the night you may speak with Lung () and PRT Trooper – ABB mole () here.

You win when all threats to the ABB’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are a PRT Trooper, and also a mole for the Azn Bad Boys. Having risen from being a common grunt, you are now a valued spy. However, times may call for you to be used for common grunt work like killing. The PRT director can order you to kill someone during the night. You have the ability to not carry out the order, and the director will not be notified of this. The order will be received before 72 hours after night begins. If you choose to perform the ordered kill then you cannot also perform the ABB factional kill.

During the night you may speak with Lung () and ABB grunt () here.

You win when all threats to the ABB’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are Skidmark, the leader of the Merchants, the greatest gang in Brockton Bay. Each night you can use your power to push someone toward another direction, causing them to target someone else. You can either target someone and cause them to target someone else you choose, or you can target someone and any actions targeting them will instead target someone else you choose. Your ability has priority over other abilities, except ones that roleblock you, unless your ability interrupts that. During the night you may speak with Merchants grunt () here.

You win when all threats to the Merchants’ control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are a Merchant grunt, a common goon working for the Merchants. Each night you may perform the factional kill for the Merchants, if it is assigned to you. During the night you may speak with Skidmark () here.

You win when all threats to the Merchants’ control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.




You are Coil, a villainous mastermind with a secret underground base. Your power lets you split the world into two separate timelines, where you can make different choices. Each night you may submit two sets of action, A and B. At the end of the night, you will be presented with two different outcomes and you have 24 hours to choose which one will become reality. Note that you will know information about what happens in the dropped timeline as well. The information you receive is any investigative results you receive, who dies and their role. If different sets of people die in the two timelines, you will know their identities, even if they didn’t die in the timeline you choose to keep. If you do not submit your choice within 24 hours, action set A will be chosen, unless you die in timeline A and you don’t die in timeline B.

During the night you can speak with Mercenary () and PRT Trooper – Coil mole () here. You do not have a factional kill.

You also have control over the Undersiders, a group of small time villains. Twice during the game you may give orders to their entire group.

The Undersiders have the following powers.

<insert>

You win when all threats to your control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated and you are alive at the end of the game.



You are a Mercenary working for Coil. You are armed with Tinkertech weaponry, and it can last through two uses. During the night, you may choose to kill someone (two shot vigilante).

During the night you may speak with Coil () and PRT Trooper – Coil mole () here.

You win when all threats to Coil’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.




You are a PRT Trooper, and also a mole working for Coil. Having risen from being a common grunt, you are now a valued spy. The PRT director can order you to kill someone during the night. You have the ability to not carry out the order, and the director will not be notified of this. The order will be received before 72 hours after night begins.

During the night you may speak with Coil () and Mercenary () here.

You win when all threats to Coil’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are Lisa Wilbourn, also known as Tattletale, a member of the small time villain group, The Undersiders. Your power gives you immense deductive abilities, and each night you may target someone and learn their faction affiliation and their abilities.

Your fellow Undersiders are Grue () and Skitter (). You may talk to them during the night here.

Also, the Undersiders work for a mysterious boss. Twice during the game, he may issue your team orders, which will replace the night actions you chose. He will receive a copy of any investigation results obtained by your team. You will be informed at the end of the night if this occurs and you will know what actions you were ordered to take.

You win if you survive to the end of the game.




You are Brian Laborn, also known as Grue, a member of the small time villain group, The Undersiders. Your power allows you to surround an area in darkness. Each night you can target someone, who will be rendered untargetable by most night actions. Certain abilities can bypass the darkness, for example that of your partner Skitter.

Your fellow Undersiders are Tattletale () and Skitter (). You may talk to them during the night here.

Also, the Undersiders work for a mysterious boss. Twice during the game, he may issue your team orders, which will replace the night actions you chose. He will receive a copy of any investigation results obtained by your team. You will be informed at the end of the night if this occurs and you will know what actions you were ordered to take.

You win if you survive to the end of the game.




You are Taylor Hebert, also known as Skitter, a member of the small time villain group, The Undersiders. Your power allows you to have absolute control over all bugs around you in a radius of several blocks. Each night you may either choose to have venomous bugs kill someone (vigilante), tie someone up in spider silk (roleblock), track someone’s movements (tracker), or watch an area (watcher). You also passively watch the area around yourself, and you will be informed of who targeted you during the night. In addition, your costume which you constructed with spider silk, provides you with some protection against attacks (1-Shot Bulletproof).

Your fellow Undersiders are Tattletale () and Grue (). You may talk to them during the night here.

Also, the Undersiders work for a mysterious boss. Twice during the game, he may issue your team orders, which will replace the night actions you chose. He will receive a copy of any investigation results obtained by your team. You will be informed at the end of the night if this occurs and you will know what actions you were ordered to take.

You win if you survive to the end of the game.




You are The Butcher. When you die, your power will pass onto the player who killed you (random if multiple) or the player who was the last vote on your hang. They will be taken over and have their power added to the collective.

You win if you die before the game ends.

[hide="Role PMs"]You are Emily Piggot, the director of the PRT ENE. You are the leader of the parahuman law enforcement in Brockton Bay. You have 5 PRT Troopers under your command, and each night you may give an order for one of them to kill someone. Unfortunately, there are likely moles among them, and some might fail to carry out their orders. The PRT troopers are ().

You can also give orders to the parahumans under your command, Armsmaster (), Battery (), and Clockblocker(). Their abilities are as follows.

<insert>

Note that each of them can willingly disobey your order and perform their own action at most once in the game. You will be informed when this occurs.

You must submit all orders in the 48 hours after the start of night.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Colin Wallis, also known as Armsmaster, a parahuman in the Protectorate ENE. You are an expert Tinker with a specialization in miniaturization who has made a large variety of powerful gear for yourself. Your power armor can allow you to survive one attack when it is deployed. Also, you have a wide variety of tools embedded within your halberd. You have an investigation tool (Cop), electronic tracker (Tracker), tranquilizer (Roleblocker), and monomolecular blade (Vigilante). One device can be used each night. Each device may only be used once after each maintenance. Once you perform actions on two nights you must perform maintenance on your equipment, which will fix it (restore 1-shot bulletproof). During the night you perform maintenance your equipment is not operational.

You take orders from the director of the PRT. If orders are sent do you, you will receive them before the 72nd hour after night begins. Once during the game you may willingly disobey your order and perform a different action.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.




You are Battery, a parahuman in the Protectorate ENE. Your power allows you to charge your ability and then use it. Each night you may either charge your ability, use your ability, or do nothing. Your ability must be charged before use. Using your ability will cause you to become immune to night actions during that night and you are able to kill someone.

You take orders from the director of the PRT. If orders are sent do you, you will receive them before the 72nd hour after night begins. Once during the game you may willingly disobey your order and perform a different action.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Clockblocker, a parahuman in the Wards ENE. Each night you may target someone with your ability and freeze them. They will be unable to take actions that night and they cannot be killed.

You take orders from the director of the PRT. If orders are sent do you, you will receive them before the 72nd hour after night begins. Once during the game you may willingly disobey your order and perform a different action.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are a PRT trooper, serving the director of the PRT ENE. You can kill someone during the night, but only if the director orders you to.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Victoria Dallon, also known as Glory Girl, a member of the independent hero team New Wave. Each night you can choose someone to kill. Also, your forcefield will protect you from being attacked, but not if you are attacked more than once on a single night or if you are attacked the night after a night where your forcefield was used.

During the night you can talk with your sister Amy () here.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Amy Dallon, also known as Panacea, a member of the independent hero team New Wave. Each night you can choose someone other than yourself to heal.

During the night you can talk with your sister Victoria () here.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.


You are a Citizen of Brockton Bay. You have no power other than your vote.

You win when all criminal elements have been eliminated from Brockton Bay.



You are Kaiser, the leader of the Empire 88, the greatest gang in Brockton Bay. Each night you may kill someone. This kill is independent from the factional kill for the E88, but you yourself cannot perform both in a single night. During the night you may speak with Othala () and PRT Trooper – E88 mole () here.

You win when all threats to the E88’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are Othala, a parahuman member of the Empire 88. Each night you may target someone other than yourself and protect them from being killed. During the night you may speak with Kaiser () and PRT Trooper – E88 mole () here.

You win when all threats to the E88’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are a PRT Trooper, and also a mole for the Empire 88. Having risen from being a common grunt, you are now a valued spy. However, times may call for you to be used for common grunt work like killing. The PRT director can order you to kill someone during the night. You have the ability to not carry out the order, and the director will not be notified of this. The order will be received before 72 hours after night begins. If you choose to perform the ordered kill then you cannot also perform the E88 factional kill.

During the night you may speak with Kaiser () and Othala () here.

You win when all threats to the E88’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are Lung, the leader of the Azn Bad Boys, the greatest gang in Brockton Bay. Each night you may kill someone. This kill is independent from the factional kill for the ABB, but you yourself cannot perform both in a single night. In addition, if you are targeted by at least two kills during a single night, you will survive both and instead kill everyone who attacked you. During the night you may speak with ABB grunt () and PRT Trooper – ABB mole () here.

You win when all threats to the ABB’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are an ABB grunt, a common goon working for the Azn Bad Boys. Each night you may perform the ABB factional kill, if it is assigned to you. During the night you may speak with Lung () and PRT Trooper – ABB mole () here.

You win when all threats to the ABB’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are a PRT Trooper, and also a mole for the Azn Bad Boys. Having risen from being a common grunt, you are now a valued spy. However, times may call for you to be used for common grunt work like killing. The PRT director can order you to kill someone during the night. You have the ability to not carry out the order, and the director will not be notified of this. The order will be received before 72 hours after night begins. If you choose to perform the ordered kill then you cannot also perform the ABB factional kill.

During the night you may speak with Lung () and ABB grunt () here.

You win when all threats to the ABB’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are Skidmark, the leader of the Merchants, the greatest gang in Brockton Bay. Each night you can use your power to push someone toward another direction, causing them to target someone else. You can either target someone and cause them to target someone else you choose, or you can target someone and any actions targeting them will instead target someone else you choose. Your ability has priority over other abilities, except ones that roleblock you, unless your ability interrupts that. During the night you may speak with Merchants grunt () here.

You win when all threats to the Merchants’ control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are a Merchant grunt, a common goon working for the Merchants. Each night you may perform the factional kill for the Merchants, if it is assigned to you. During the night you may speak with Skidmark () here.

You win when all threats to the Merchants’ control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.




You are Coil, a villainous mastermind with a secret underground base. Your power lets you split the world into two separate timelines, where you can make different choices. Each night you may submit two sets of action, A and B. At the end of the night, you will be presented with two different outcomes and you have 24 hours to choose which one will become reality. Note that you will know information about what happens in the dropped timeline as well. The information you receive is any investigative results you receive, who dies and their role. If different sets of people die in the two timelines, you will know their identities, even if they didn’t die in the timeline you choose to keep. If you do not submit your choice within 24 hours, action set A will be chosen, unless you die in timeline A and you don’t die in timeline B.

During the night you can speak with Mercenary () and PRT Trooper – Coil mole () here. You do not have a factional kill.

You also have control over the Undersiders, a group of small time villains. Twice during the game you may give orders to their entire group.

The Undersiders have the following powers.

<insert>

You win when all threats to your control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated and you are alive at the end of the game.



You are a Mercenary working for Coil. You are armed with Tinkertech weaponry, and it can last through two uses. During the night, you may choose to kill someone (two shot vigilante).

During the night you may speak with Coil () and PRT Trooper – Coil mole () here.

You win when all threats to Coil’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.




You are a PRT Trooper, and also a mole working for Coil. Having risen from being a common grunt, you are now a valued spy. The PRT director can order you to kill someone during the night. You have the ability to not carry out the order, and the director will not be notified of this. The order will be received before 72 hours after night begins.

During the night you may speak with Coil () and Mercenary () here.

You win when all threats to Coil’s control over Brockton Bay have been eliminated.



You are Lisa Wilbourn, also known as Tattletale, a member of the small time villain group, The Undersiders. Your power gives you immense deductive abilities, and each night you may target someone and learn their faction affiliation and their abilities.

Your fellow Undersiders are Grue () and Skitter (). You may talk to them during the night here.

Also, the Undersiders work for a mysterious boss. Twice during the game, he may issue your team orders, which will replace the night actions you chose. He will receive a copy of any investigation results obtained by your team. You will be informed at the end of the night if this occurs and you will know what actions you were ordered to take.

You win if you survive to the end of the game.




You are Brian Laborn, also known as Grue, a member of the small time villain group, The Undersiders. Your power allows you to surround an area in darkness. Each night you can target someone, who will be rendered untargetable by most night actions. Certain abilities can bypass the darkness, for example that of your partner Skitter.

Your fellow Undersiders are Tattletale () and Skitter (). You may talk to them during the night here.

Also, the Undersiders work for a mysterious boss. Twice during the game, he may issue your team orders, which will replace the night actions you chose. He will receive a copy of any investigation results obtained by your team. You will be informed at the end of the night if this occurs and you will know what actions you were ordered to take.

You win if you survive to the end of the game.




You are Taylor Hebert, also known as Skitter, a member of the small time villain group, The Undersiders. Your power allows you to have absolute control over all bugs around you in a radius of several blocks. Each night you may either choose to have venomous bugs kill someone (vigilante), tie someone up in spider silk (roleblock), track someone’s movements (tracker), or watch an area (watcher). You also passively watch the area around yourself, and you will be informed of who targeted you during the night. In addition, your costume which you constructed with spider silk, provides you with some protection against attacks (1-Shot Bulletproof).

Your fellow Undersiders are Tattletale () and Grue (). You may talk to them during the night here.

Also, the Undersiders work for a mysterious boss. Twice during the game, he may issue your team orders, which will replace the night actions you chose. He will receive a copy of any investigation results obtained by your team. You will be informed at the end of the night if this occurs and you will know what actions you were ordered to take.

You win if you survive to the end of the game.




You are The Butcher. When you die, your power will pass onto the player who killed you (random if multiple) or the player who was the last vote on your hang. They will be taken over and have their power added to the collective.

You win if you die before the game ends.
